attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a box with a remote in it on the couch.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Agent: A robotic agent is present in the scene, though not visually depicted. It is initially not located at any object’s position.
   - Receptacles:
     - Cardboard Box: A large, open, brown cardboard box placed on the coffee table. It is openable and currently opened. It is empty and can hold objects.
     - Couch: A green-and-white checkered couch with two visible cushions. It functions as a receptacle since it can hold objects (e.g., the box). It is not openable.
   - Objects:
     - Remote Control: Two remote controls are present on the coffee table. One is gray with blue buttons, and the other is silver with yellow and blue buttons. Both are small, rectangular, and have button arrays.
     - Small Green Box: A small, dark green rectangular box with a white object (possibly a controller or device) inside. It is on the coffee table.
     - Green Cup: A small, light green cylindrical cup on the coffee table.
     - Round Gold Cushion: A round, gold-colored cushion with star patterns, resting on the right side of the couch.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 Agent (implied, not visible)
   - 2 Receptacles: 1 cardboard box, 1 couch
   - 4 Objects: 2 remote controls, 1 small green box, 1 green cup
   - 1 additional object: 1 round gold cushion (on couch, not a receptacle)

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The cardboard box is on the coffee table, open, and positioned toward the left side of the table.
   - The two remote controls are on the coffee table to the right of the cardboard box.
   - The small green box is between the cardboard box and the remotes.
   - The green cup is near the top edge of the coffee table, slightly left of center.
   - The couch is behind the coffee table, occupying the upper portion of the scene.
   - The round gold cushion is on the right cushion of the couch.
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to Planning Task:
   - The cardboard box is opened and empty.
   - The remotes are not held by the agent and are not in any receptacle.
   - The couch is not openable and is currently empty (except for the cushion, which is not relevant to the task).
   - No object is currently being held by the agent.
   - No object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled — these states are irrelevant to the current instruction.

5. Visual Properties:
   - Cardboard Box: Light brown, open flaps, rectangular, approximately 1/3 the width of the coffee table.
   - Remote Controls: One is gray with blue buttons; the other is silver with yellow and blue buttons. Both are approximately the same size.
   - Small Green Box: Dark green, rectangular, with a white object inside, positioned near the center of the table.
   - Green Cup: Light green, cylindrical, small, located near the top edge of the table.
   - Couch: Green and white checkered pattern, two visible cushions, occupies the upper half of the image.
   - Round Gold Cushion: Gold with star patterns, circular, on the right cushion of the couch.
   - Coffee Table: Light beige, rectangular, with a flat surface, supporting the box, remotes, green box, and cup.
   - Floor: Light brown, textured carpet.

The task requires the agent to pick up one remote, place it in the cardboard box, and then place the box (with the remote inside) on the couch. The box is currently open and empty, making it ready to receive an object. The couch is accessible and can serve as the final receptacle for the box.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_remote_in_box_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cardboard_box - receptacle
        couch - receptacle
        remote1 - object
        remote2 - object
        small_green_box - object
        green_cup - object
        round_gold_cushion - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 cardboard_box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 couch))
        (not (atLocation agent1 remote1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 remote2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 small_green_box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 green_cup))
        (not (atLocation agent1 round_gold_cushion))
        (not (atLocation agent1 coffee_table))

        ; Cardboard box is openable and opened
        (openable cardboard_box)
        (opened cardboard_box)

        ; Couch is not openable
        (not (openable couch))

        ; Objects are on the coffee table
        (atLocation cardboard_box coffee_table)
        (atLocation remote1 coffee_table)
        (atLocation remote2 coffee_table)
        (atLocation small_green_box coffee_table)
        (atLocation green_cup coffee_table)

        ; Couch has the cushion on it (not relevant to task, but present)
        (atLocation round_gold_cushion couch)

        ; No object is held by agent
        (not (holdsAny agent1))

        ; No object is in any receptacle initially (except cushion on couch, but it's not a receptacle object)
        (not (inReceptacle remote1 cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle remote2 cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle small_green_box cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle green_cup cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch))

        ; No cleaning, heating, cooling, slicing, or toggling states are relevant
        ; (isClean, isHot, isCool, isSliced, isOn, isToggled are not required for this task)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The cardboard box is on the couch
            (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch)
            ; One remote is inside the cardboard box
            (or
                (inReceptacle remote1 cardboard_box)
                (inReceptacle remote2 cardboard_box)
            )
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_remote_in_box_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cardboard_box - receptacle
        couch - receptacle
        remote1 - object
        remote2 - object
        small_green_box - object
        green_cup - object
        round_gold_cushion - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 cardboard_box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 couch))
        (not (atLocation agent1 remote1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 remote2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 small_green_box))
        (not (atLocation agent1 green_cup))
        (not (atLocation agent1 round_gold_cushion))
        (not (atLocation agent1 coffee_table))

        ; Cardboard box is openable and opened
        (openable cardboard_box)
        (opened cardboard_box)

        ; Couch is not openable
        (not (openable couch))

        ; Objects are on the coffee table
        (atLocation cardboard_box coffee_table)
        (atLocation remote1 coffee_table)
        (atLocation remote2 coffee_table)
        (atLocation small_green_box coffee_table)
        (atLocation green_cup coffee_table)

        ; Couch has the cushion on it (not relevant to task, but present)
        (atLocation round_gold_cushion couch)

        ; No object is held by agent
        (not (holdsAny agent1))

        ; No object is in any receptacle initially (except cushion on couch, but it's not a receptacle object)
        (not (inReceptacle remote1 cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle remote2 cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle small_green_box cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle green_cup cardboard_box))
        (not (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch))

        ; No cleaning, heating, cooling, slicing, or toggling states are relevant
        ; (isClean, isHot, isCool, isSliced, isOn, isToggled are not required for this task)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The cardboard box is on the couch
            (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch)
            ; One remote is inside the cardboard box
            (or
                (inReceptacle remote1 cardboard_box)
                (inReceptacle remote2 cardboard_box)
            )
        )
    )
)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a box with a remote in it on the couch.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a living room area with a couch and a coffee table. The primary objects relevant to the task are a cardboard box, a remote control, and a couch.

1. Objects and their types:
   - The couch is a large, upholstered piece of furniture with a green and white checkered pattern. It is positioned against the back wall and spans most of the upper portion of the image. In the context of the domain, the couch is treated as a receptacle because it can hold objects (e.g., the box).
   - The cardboard box is open and placed on the coffee table. It is a light brown, rectangular box with flaps open, indicating it is currently unsealed. It is a receptacle as it can contain other objects.
   - The remote control is a small, gray, rectangular device with buttons and is located on the coffee table. It is an object (not a receptacle). There are two remote controls visible in some views, but only one is needed for the task. The remote has a blue button panel and a yellow top section.
   - The coffee table is a light wooden table with a flat top and four legs. It is not a receptacle in the domain, but it serves as a surface where objects are placed. It is positioned in front of the couch.

2. Quantities:
   - One couch (receptacle)
   - One open cardboard box (receptacle)
   - Two remote controls (objects) — but only one is required for the task
   - One coffee table (object, not a receptacle)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The cardboard box is on the coffee table.
   - The remote control is on the coffee table, next to the box.
   - The couch is behind the coffee table.
   - The box is not yet on the couch, and the remote is not yet inside the box.
   - The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - The box is open (implied by its visual state) but not yet containing the remote.
   - The remote is on the table and not held by the agent.
   - The couch is closed (not opened) and empty — it can receive the box.
   - The agent is not holding any object (holdsAny is false).
   - No objects are currently in any receptacle (inReceptacle is false for all).

5. Visual properties:
   - The couch has a distinct green and white grid pattern with a decorative golden cushion on the right side.
   - The cardboard box is plain brown with visible flaps.
   - The remote control is gray with a blue button area and yellow top; it is positioned to the right of the box on the table.
   - The coffee table has a light wood finish and a glass or glossy top.
   - The floor is a uniform beige carpet.

The task requires the robot to pick up the remote, place it inside the box, then pick up the box (with the remote inside), and place the box on the couch. The couch must be treated as a receptacle to accept the box. The box must be open to receive the remote.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_box_with_remote_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - receptacle
        box - receptacle
        remote - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table) ; robot starts at coffee table
        (inReceptacle box coffee_table) ; box is on coffee table
        (inReceptacle remote coffee_table) ; remote is on coffee table
        (openable box) ; box is openable (and visually open)
        (opened box) ; box is already open
        (not (holdsAny robot)) ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle remote box)) ; remote is not in box
        (not (inReceptacle box couch)) ; box is not on couch
        (not (opened couch)) ; couch is not opened (though it doesn't need to be for this task)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle remote box) ; remote is inside the box
            (inReceptacle box couch) ; box (with remote) is on the couch
        )
    )
)
```

 (:requirements

 )
 (:types
  agent

    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a living room area with a couch and a coffee table. The primary objects relevant to the task are a cardboard box, a remote control, and a couch.

1. Objects and their types:
   - The couch is a large, upholstered piece of furniture with a green and white checkered pattern. It is positioned against the back wall and spans most of the upper portion of the image. In the context of the domain, the couch is treated as a receptacle because it can hold objects (e.g., the box).
   - The cardboard box is open and placed on the coffee table. It is a light brown, rectangular box with flaps open, indicating it is currently unsealed. It is a receptacle as it can contain other objects.
   - The remote control is a small, gray, rectangular device with buttons and is located on the coffee table. It is an object (not a receptacle). There are two remote controls visible in some views, but only one is needed for the task. The remote has a blue button panel and a yellow top section.
   - The coffee table is a light wooden table with a flat top and four legs. It is not a receptacle in the domain, but it serves as a surface where objects are placed. It is positioned in front of the couch.

2. Quantities:
   - One couch (receptacle)
   - One open cardboard box (receptacle)
   - Two remote controls (objects) — but only one is required for the task
   - One coffee table (object, not a receptacle)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The cardboard box is on the coffee table.
   - The remote control is on the coffee table, next to the box.
   - The couch is behind the coffee table.
   - The box is not yet on the couch, and the remote is not yet inside the box.
   - The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - The box is open (implied by its visual state) but not yet containing the remote.
   - The remote is on the table and not held by the agent.
   - The couch is closed (not opened) and empty — it can receive the box.
   - The agent is not holding any object (holdsAny is false).
   - No objects are currently in any receptacle (inReceptacle is false for all).

5. Visual properties:
   - The couch has a distinct green and white grid pattern with a decorative golden cushion on the right side.
   - The cardboard box is plain brown with visible flaps.
   - The remote control is gray with a blue button area and yellow top; it is positioned to the right of the box on the table.
   - The coffee table has a light wood finish and a glass or glossy top.
   - The floor is a uniform beige carpet.

The task requires the robot to pick up the remote, place it inside the box, then pick up the box (with the remote inside), and place the box on the couch. The couch must be treated as a receptacle to accept the box. The box must be open to receive the remote.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.

Generate the PDDL problem:
final.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_box_with_remote_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - receptacle
        box - receptacle
        remote - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table) ; robot starts at coffee table
        (inReceptacle box coffee_table) ; box is on coffee table
        (inReceptacle remote coffee_table) ; remote is on coffee table
        (openable box) ; box is openable (and visually open)
        (opened box) ; box is already open
        (not (holdsAny robot)) ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle remote box)) ; remote is not in box
        (not (inReceptacle box couch)) ; box is not on couch
        (not (opened couch)) ; couch is not opened (though it doesn't need to be for this task)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle remote box) ; remote is inside the box
            (inReceptacle box couch) ; box (with remote) is on the couch
        )
    )
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem put_box_with_remote_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - receptacle
        box - receptacle
        remote - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table) ; robot starts at coffee table
        (inReceptacle box coffee_table) ; box is on coffee table
        (inReceptacle remote coffee_table) ; remote is on coffee table
        (openable box) ; box is openable (and visually open)
        (opened box) ; box is already open
        (not (holdsAny robot)) ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle remote box)) ; remote is not in box
        (not (inReceptacle box couch)) ; box is not on couch
        (not (opened couch)) ; couch is not opened (though it doesn't need to be for this task)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle remote box) ; remote is inside the box
            (inReceptacle box couch) ; box (with remote) is on the couch
        )
    )
)

(pickupobject robot remote)
(gotolocation robot box)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot remote box)

(gotolocation robot couch)
